summary: Rewrote the v3 plan to a higher standard using fresh 2026 research + in-repo 2.2.0 assets. 17 children in 5 waves. Five research-driven corrections to the prior plan: (1) adopt pnpm+Turborepo+Changesets+tsup (prior punted Turborepo); (2) use official rmcp Rust SDK + Streamable HTTP for MCP (prior unspecified); (3) Riverpod 3.0 codegen (prior generic); (4) tauri-specta v2 typed bindings (prior untyped); (5) CRDT sync = Yjs default + Loro option reusing the existing 2.2.0 createLoroMergeStrategy seam (prior Yjs-only, ignored shipped asset). Also credits 2.2.0 assets (IncrementalView, time-travel, Tauri SQLite adapter, AG-UI bridge) to reduce effort. Critical path: monorepo → core-extraction → {svelte canary, react-parity} in parallel with sdl → rust-cli → mcp/a2a.
open_questions: Confirm Turborepo adoption now vs later (plan recommends now; reversible)
Confirm v3.0.0 ships as a re-export shim so the umbrella npm path stays working through the monorepo split
recommended_next_stage: execute (or kbd-analyze first if a build-vs-adopt candidate ledger is wanted for the Rust/Flutter/CRDT deps)
